# @acepad/todo

A reactive, tree-structured Todo application library built with a custom React-like component system. It renders directly to the DOM using a lightweight set of `@hoge1e3/*` libraries — no virtual DOM, no build step required for the runtime.

---

## Overview

`@acepad/todo` is a self-contained todo app module that:

- Parses a plain-text, indented `todo.txt` file into a reactive tree of `Item` objects.
- Renders the tree as a nested `<ul>/<li>` structure in the browser.
- Supports **inline editing** of titles and **checkbox toggling** for completion state.
- Supports **expand/collapse** of subtasks by clicking on a list item.
- Has a **"show completed"** toggle to filter out finished tasks.
- Uses [immer](https://immerjs.github.io/immer/) for immutable state updates, and a lightweight reactive ref system for change propagation.

---

## Data Model (`todo.ts`)

### Interface: `Item`

```typescript
export interface Item {
  id: string;      // Auto-generated unique ID (runtime only, not persisted)
  title: string;   // The text content of the todo item
  ok?: boolean;    // true if the item is marked as completed
  sub: Item[];     // Child items (nested subtasks)
}
```

> **Note:** `id` is generated at decode time using `@hoge1e3/dom-id` and is not stored in `todo.txt`. It is used at runtime to track and update individual items reactively.

---

### Function: `decode(text)`

```typescript
export function decode(text: string): Item[]
```

Parses an indented plain-text string into a tree of `Item` objects.

**Rules:**
- Lines are split by newline (`\n` or `\r\n`). Empty lines are ignored.
- Leading spaces determine the nesting depth. Each additional level of indentation creates a child item.
- A line starting with `ok ` (followed by a space) is marked as completed (`ok: true`), and the prefix is stripped from the title.
- Each item is assigned a unique `id` via `@hoge1e3/dom-id`.

**Example:**

```
Buy groceries
  Milk
  ok Eggs
Write report
```

Produces:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "...",
    "title": "Buy groceries",
    "sub": [
      { "id": "...", "title": "Milk", "sub": [] },
      { "id": "...", "title": "Eggs", "ok": true, "sub": [] }
    ]
  },
  { "id": "...", "title": "Write report", "sub": [] }
]
```

---

### Function: `encode(data)`

```typescript
export function encode(data: Item[]): string
```

Serializes a tree of `Item` objects back to an indented plain-text string.

- Each level of nesting is represented by one additional leading space.
- Completed items (`ok: true`) are prefixed with `"ok "`.
- The output is joined with `\n`.

**Example:**

```typescript
encode([
  { id: "1", title: "Buy groceries", sub: [
    { id: "2", title: "Milk", sub: [] },
    { id: "3", title: "Eggs", ok: true, sub: [] }
  ]},
])
// Returns:
// "Buy groceries\n Milk\n ok Eggs"
```

---

### Function: `findById(items, id)`

```typescript
export function findById(items: Item[], id: string): Item | null
```

Recursively searches a tree of `Item` objects for the item with the given `id`.

- Performs a depth-first search: checks the current level first, then recurses into `sub`.
- Returns `null` if no item with the given `id` is found.

---

## UI Layer (`ui.ts`)

The UI is rendered as a reactive DOM tree. It uses `this.widget(wmain)` from the host environment (the `acepad` runtime), which mounts the component into the page.

### Entry Point: `main()`

```typescript
export async function main(this: any): Promise<HTMLElement>
```

The top-level entry point called by the acepad runtime. It calls `this.widget(wmain)` to mount the root widget component.

---

### Component: `wmain`

```typescript
function wmain(this: any, { id, t }: { id: Idgen, t: TagGenerator }): HTMLElement
```

The root widget. Responsibilities:

- Reads `todo.txt` from the runtime filesystem via `this.resolve(this.$home).rel("todo.txt").text()`.
- Decodes it into a reactive `rootRef` (`PathRef<Item[]>`) using `@hoge1e3/immer-path-ref`.
- Manages a `showok` ref (boolean) to toggle visibility of completed items.
- Manages an `expandedRef` map (`PathRef<Record<string, true>>`) to track which items are expanded.
- Renders a `<div>` containing:
  - A checkbox (bound to `showok`) that toggles display of completed items.
  - The root `todoListComponent`.

---

### Component: `todoListComponent`

```typescript
function todoListComponent(id: Idgen, items: PathRef<Item[]>): HTMLElement
```

Renders a `<ul>` containing one `todoComponent` per item.

- Uses `@hoge1e3/component`'s `c()` to reactively re-render when `items` or `showok` changes.
- Filters out completed items when `showok` is `false`.
- For each item, creates a `todoComponent` using a narrowed `PathRef` pointing to that specific item (via `findById`).

---

### Component: `todoComponent`

```typescript
function todoComponent(
  item: ReadonlyPathRef<Item>,
  expanded: PathRef<boolean>
): HTMLElement
```

Renders a single `<li>` for a todo item. It is reactively updated when `item` or `expanded` changes.

- Displays a `"+"` span if the item has subtasks.
- Contains:
  - A **checkbox** bound to `item.ok` via `refInput` — toggling it updates the reactive state.
  - A **text input** bound to `item.title` via `refInput` — editing it updates the reactive state.
  - A nested `todoListComponent` for `item.sub`, shown only when `expanded` is `true`.
- Clicking the `<li>` (but not on an `<input>`) toggles the `expanded` state.

---

## Dependency Libraries

### @hoge1e3/ref

A minimal reactive reference library, similar in spirit to Vue's `ref` / `computed`.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `ref<T>(val: T): Ref<T>` | Creates a mutable reactive reference holding a value of type `T`. Reading `.value` returns the current value; setting `.value` fires a `"change"` event. |
| `computed(...refs, fn): ReadonlyRef<T>` | Creates a derived read-only reference whose value is recomputed from one or more source refs whenever any of them change. |
| `custom<T>(get, set): Ref<T>` | Creates a ref with custom getter and setter logic. Used internally by `immer-path-ref`. |

**`Ref<T>` interface:**

```typescript
interface Ref<T> extends ReadonlyRef<T> {
  value: T;            // Read or write the current value
  set(v: T): void;     // Equivalent to .value = v
}

interface ReadonlyRef<T> {
  readonly value: T;
  get(): T;
  on(event: "change", handler: (e: { prev: T; val: T }) => void): Remover;
  fire(event: string, payload: unknown): void;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
import { ref, computed } from "@hoge1e3/ref";

const count = ref(0);
const doubled = computed(count, (n) => n * 2);

count.on("change", ({ prev, val }) => console.log(prev, "->", val));

count.value = 5;      // logs: 0 -> 5
console.log(doubled.value); // 10
```

---

### @hoge1e3/immer-path-ref

Wraps a `Ref<T>` with [immer](https://immerjs.github.io/immer/)-powered deep path access, enabling reactive reads and writes to nested properties of an immutable state tree.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `create<T>(r: Ref<T>): PathRef<T>` | Wraps a root `Ref` as a `PathRef`, enabling `.join()` to traverse nested properties. |

**`PathRef<T>` / `ReadonlyPathRef<T>`:**

- Extends `Ref<T>` (or `ReadonlyRef<T>`) with a `.join(path)` method.
- `.join(key)` — where `key` is a property name string — returns a `PathRef<T[key]>` pointing to that nested property.
- `.join(fn)` — where `fn` is a function `(t: T) => N` — returns a `ReadonlyPathRef<N>` for derived/computed paths.
- Writing to a `PathRef` triggers an immer `produce()` call on the root ref, preserving immutability.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";
import { create } from "@hoge1e3/immer-path-ref";

const state = ref({ user: { name: "Alice", age: 30 } });
const stateRef = create(state);

const nameRef = stateRef.join("user").join("name");
console.log(nameRef.value); // "Alice"

nameRef.value = "Bob";
console.log(state.value.user.name); // "Bob"
```

---

### @hoge1e3/dom

A lightweight helper for creating DOM elements using a fluent, JSX-like syntax — without any transpilation.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `t: TagGenerator` | A pre-created `TagGenerator` instance (Proxy). Access any property as a tag name. |
| `tagGenerator(): TagGenerator` | Creates a new `TagGenerator` instance. |

**Usage:**

```typescript
import { t } from "@hoge1e3/dom";

const el = t.div(
  { style: "color: blue;" },
  "Hello, ",
  t.button(
    { onclick() { alert("clicked!"); } },
    "Click me"
  )
);
// Returns: <div style="color: blue;">Hello, <button>Click me</button></div>
// with the click event listener already attached.
document.body.appendChild(el);
```

**Attribute rules:**
- Any key starting with `on` is treated as an event listener and registered via `addEventListener`.
- Other keys are set as attributes or properties on the element.
- Children can be `HTMLElement`, `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`, or `undefined`. Primitive values become text nodes.

---

### @hoge1e3/dom-id

Generates hierarchical, unique string IDs for DOM elements, enabling nested components to manage their own ID namespaces.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `id(pre?: string): Idgen` | Creates a root `Idgen` with an optional prefix. |

**`Idgen` interface:**

An `Idgen` is both callable and has dynamic property access. Each property access creates a child ID scope. Calling it converts it to a string (the actual ID value).

```typescript
import { id } from "@hoge1e3/dom-id";

const wid = id("widget");
console.log(wid.root + "");    // e.g. "widget-root-1"
console.log(wid.root.item + ""); // e.g. "widget-root-1-item-2"
```

In components, `Idgen` instances are passed in and used as element `id` attributes, ensuring each rendered element has a stable, unique ID that `@hoge1e3/component` can use to find and update it in the DOM.

---

### @hoge1e3/component

A reactive DOM component helper. Given a set of `Ref` inputs and a render function, it produces an `HTMLElement` and automatically re-renders (in-place in the DOM) whenever the inputs change.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `c(id, ...refs, renderFn)` | Creates or updates a component. |

**Signature:**

```typescript
function c<T extends HTMLElement>(
  id: Idgen,
  ...args: [...ReadonlyRef<unknown>[], (...values: unknown[]) => T]
): T
```

**Behavior:**

1. On first call, calls `renderFn(...currentValues)`, sets the element's `id`, and registers a `computed` watcher on all refs.
2. On subsequent calls (or when a ref changes), if the values have not changed since the last render, it returns the cached element.
3. If values have changed, it calls `renderFn` again and uses `@hoge1e3/dom-updater`'s `update()` to patch the live DOM element in-place, avoiding full replacement.
4. When the element is removed from the DOM, the watcher is automatically cleaned up.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";
import { t } from "@hoge1e3/dom";
import { id } from "@hoge1e3/dom-id";
import { c } from "@hoge1e3/component";

const wid = id("my");
const count = ref(0);

const el = c(wid.counter, count, (n) => t.span(`Count: ${n}`));
document.body.appendChild(el);

count.value++;  // The <span> in the DOM is updated automatically.
```

---

### @hoge1e3/ref-input

Binds a `Ref` (or `PathRef`) to an `HTMLInputElement`, keeping the ref value and the input value in two-way sync.

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `refInput<T extends HTMLElement>(ref, input): T` | Binds `ref` to `input` and returns the input element. |

**Behavior:**
- When `ref.value` changes, the input's `value` (or `checked` for checkboxes) is updated.
- When the user edits the input, `ref.value` is updated via the appropriate DOM event.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";
import { t } from "@hoge1e3/dom";
import { refInput } from "@hoge1e3/ref-input";

const name = ref("Alice");
const input = refInput(name, t.input({ type: "text" }));
document.body.appendChild(input);
// Editing the input updates name.value, and name.value = "Bob" updates the input.
```

---

## Data Format: `todo.txt`

The application reads from a plain-text file called `todo.txt` in the user's home directory on the acepad filesystem. The format is:

```
Task title
  Subtask (indented by spaces)
  Another subtask
    Deeply nested subtask
  ok Completed subtask
ok Completed top-level task
```

**Rules:**
- Indentation (any number of leading spaces) determines nesting depth.
- A line starting with `ok ` (with a space) marks the item as completed.
- Empty lines are ignored.
- The file is encoded back to this format by `encode()` when saving.

---

## Testing

```bash
npm test
```

The test script (`test/test-todo.js`) reads `todo.txt` from the acepad runtime filesystem, decodes it to an `Item[]` tree, re-encodes it, and returns the result — verifying that a round-trip of `decode` → `encode` is consistent.

```typescript
import * as todo from "@acepad/todo";

export async function main() {
  const d = todo.decode(this.resolve("/idb/run/todo.txt").text());
  const e = todo.encode(d);
  return e;
}
```
